methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Frankenburg Technologies is a Series A-stage defense and security company that has raised €40M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 private and 2 public companies operating in the Defense & Security sector, including peers such as Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

The peer-median method was the applicable triangulation approach. The analysis examined 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables, which yielded a median valuation of $300M. The peer range spans from $150M (Ares Industries) to $500M (Stark Defence), with Vatn Systems at the midpoint. This peer-median result of $300M serves as the primary valuation anchor.

The triangulated valuation range is $255M to $345M, with a median point of $300M. This single-method analysis carries inherent limitations: valuation relies on a narrow peer set of three companies, and the wide dispersion within that set ($150M–$500M) reflects meaningful heterogeneity in comparable financing outcomes. Key uncertainties include the degree to which these three peers match Frankenburg's technology maturity, customer traction, and defensibility profile. A more robust estimate would require additional valuation methods (revenue multiples, DCF, or venture method) and a larger peer sample to triangulate confidence intervals around the $300M median.
